articles.description: Obey Musiwa-Herald Reporter As climate pressures intensify across Zimbabwe, farmers and Government stakeholders gathered at Mudoti Farm in Chikomba West Constituency for the provincial field day, aiming to strengthen productivity...

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Shumba Maasai Brings Zimbabwean Cultural Interventions to Venice Biennale with Procession Across Historic Campos
more.description: Zimbabwean multidisciplinary artist Shumba Maasai led a day of cultural interventions across Venice's historic campos on 9 May, culminating at the Zimbabwe Pavilion at the 61st Venice Biennale — turning public squares into temporary Shona dare spaces of dialogue, storytelling and ceremony.

more.title: Mnangagwa completes long-delayed Sibanda Zanu-PF politburo appointment
more.description: President Emmerson Mnangagwa has formally appointed retired army commander Philip Valerio Sibanda to the ZANU-PF Politburo following his retirement from the military, reviving a move that previously triggered constitutional controversy. The post Mnangagwa completes long-delayed Sibanda Zanu-PF politburo appointment appeared first on Nehanda Radio .

more.title: Police officer & Minister Motsoaledi’s cars kill foreign Couple: Zimbabwean family wiped out in 2 separate accidents along N1 Highway
more.description: The N1 Double Tragedy: A Family Wiped Out and the Perilous Life of Zimbabweans in South Africa POLOKWANE – The stretch of the N1 highway near Bela-Bela in Limpopo is often a bustling artery for travellers, but on Saturday, 9 May 2026, it became a site of unimaginable grief and a stark symbol of the [...] The post Police officer & Minister Motsoaledi’s cars kill foreign Couple: Zimbabwean family wiped out in 2 separate accidents along N1 Highway first appeared on My Zimbabwe News .

more.title: The price of Chivayo’s Fortuner: Why Zimpapers drew a line in the sand
more.description: Zimbabwe Newspapers Group has sparked debate after reportedly ordering staff at Capitalk FM to return most of the cash gifts received from businessman Wicknell Chivayo. more.title: Zimpapers orders staff to return Chivayo cash gifts, gives presenter vehicle ultimatum
more.description: Zimbabwe Newspapers Group has instructed Capitalk FM staff to return most of the cash gifts distributed by businessman Wicknell Chivayo, while presenter Phathisani Sibanda has reportedly been told to reject a luxury vehicle offer or leave the company. The post Zimpapers orders staff to return Chivayo cash gifts, gives presenter vehicle ultimatum appeared first on Nehanda Radio .

more.title: Zimbabwe to review lithium export quotas next year as producers set up processing plants
more.description: Xinhua The Zimbabwean government will review export quotas granted to six major lithium producers in the country next year once mining companies establish acceptable beneficiation capacity, a cabinet minister said on Tuesday. Responding during a media briefing in Zimbabwe’s capital of Harare, Mines and Mining Development Minister Polite Kambamura said the quotas were granted after [...] The post Zimbabwe to review lithium export quotas next year as producers set up processing plants appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Who is funding anti-migrant protests in SA? – Zimbabwean international relations expert urges probe into organisers
more.description: IOL The South African government must investigate who is driving and funding ongoing protests targeting illegal foreign nationals, as well as the broader agenda behind the demonstrations, says Zimbabwean political analyst and international relations expert Gideon Chitanga. The comments come amid recent protests in Gauteng and KwaZulu-Natal targeting undocumented immigrants. “To be fair, the issue of [...] The post Who is funding anti-migrant protests in SA? – Zimbabwean international relations expert urges probe into organisers appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Tourism boost as Zimbabwe hosts Africa Skills Week
more.description: ZIMBABWE is poised to reap significant tourism benefits as it prepares to host the African Skills Week (ASW) later this year. African Skills Week is an annual African Union (AU) initiative aimed at transforming skills development, strengthening Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET), and promoting employment opportunities across the continent. The initiative seeks to [...] The post Tourism boost as Zimbabwe hosts Africa Skills Week appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Police hunt drug kingpins linked to armed robberies
more.description: Police have launched a nationwide manhunt for three alleged drug kingpins accused of running syndicates from Harare suburbs, after explosives linked to armed robberies were recovered during investigations. The Zimbabwe Republic Police on Saturday identified the wanted suspects as Nomore Chabata, also known as “Nomara”, Joymore Chihwai and Leon Mambohaatumwi. Police spokesperson Commissioner Paul Nyathi [...] The post Police hunt drug kingpins linked to armed robberies appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Millennium Heights apartments sell out fast as demand surges
more.description: Millennium Heights is rapidly taking shape, with construction progressing at pace on its planned 1,000 apartments and demand significantly outstripping supply. Block 6, comprising 65 units, sold out before construction had even begun and is now officially under development. Following closely behind, Block 7, which offers 41 units, is already attracting strong interest from buyers [...] The post Millennium Heights apartments sell out fast as demand surges appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .
